SYLLABUS: DESCRIPTION

This course provides a broad overview of computer science. It covers the evolution of computing, basic hardware and software components, data representation, binary systems, and the fundamental role of computers in modern society.

This course focuses on algorithmic problem-solving and foundational programming concepts. It covers basic syntax, data types, control structures, and functions using a high-level programming language to build simple applications.

This course introduces the fundamental concepts of limits, derivatives, and integrals of functions of a single variable. It covers differentiation rules, applications of derivatives, optimization, and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.

This course introduces complex programming paradigms and problem-solving techniques. It covers advanced data types, memory management, file handling, basic error handling, and an introduction to modular programming.

This course explores how data is organized and manipulated efficiently. It covers implementation and analysis of core structures like arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, alongside fundamental searching and sorting algorithms.

This course advances the study of single-variable calculus with a focus on integral calculus and infinite series. It covers techniques of integration, applications of definite integrals, polar coordinates, and power series.

This course introduces the core concepts of database systems and data modeling. It covers Entity-Relationship (ER) modeling, relational database design, normalization, and the use of Structured Query Language (SQL) for data retrieval and manipulation.

This course focuses on programming paradigms where the flow of execution is determined by user actions or system events. It covers graphical user interface (GUI) design, event handlers, listeners, and asynchronous programming.
This course provides the mathematical foundations necessary for computer science and information technology. It covers logic, set theory, combinatorics, graph theory, and Boolean algebra.

This course focuses on the end-to-end process of building software applications. It covers web, mobile, or desktop development, emphasizing user interfaces, back-end integration, and deploying functional software.

This course explores mathematical and statistical techniques used for decision-making and problem-solving in business and technology. It covers probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and linear programming.

This course examines the principles and technologies underlying modern web application development and deployment. It covers client-side scripting, server-side programming, web services, and database integration.

This course introduces the foundational concepts, protocols, and architecture of computer networks. It covers the OSI model, TCP/IP suite, IP addressing, routing fundamentals, and local area network (LAN) technologies.

This course explores complex database concepts beyond basic relational models and query languages. It covers transaction management, concurrency control, distributed databases, data warehousing, and NoSQL technologies.

This course examines the hardware and software components that form the infrastructure of computing systems. It covers operating system principles, hardware architectures, virtualization, and cloud computing platforms.

This course builds upon basic networking principles to explore advanced routing and switching technologies. It covers wide area network (WAN) protocols, network security, scaling networks, and network administration.

This course introduces the principles of protecting information systems from unauthorized access, use, and destruction. It covers cryptography, risk management, access control, network security, and security policies.

This course focuses on the methods used to connect disparate software applications and systems. It covers application programming interfaces (APIs), middleware, data exchange formats, and enterprise application integration.

This course provides supervised, practical work experience within a professional industry setting. It covers the application of theoretical knowledge to real-world tasks, professional communication, and workplace ethics.

This course initiates the final, comprehensive project demonstrating the student's accumulated knowledge in the program. It covers project conceptualization, requirement gathering, feasibility analysis, and system design.

This course introduces the principles of designing and integrating complex enterprise IT systems. It covers enterprise architecture frameworks, system lifecycles, requirement analysis, and basic integration patterns.

This course examines the processes and disciplines required to ensure and maintain the quality of software systems. It covers software quality control, quality assurance practices, project management, configuration management, and release management.

This course concludes the comprehensive project begun in Capstone Project 1 through system development and deployment. It covers system implementation, software testing, user training, and final project documentation.

This course advances the study of enterprise architecture with an emphasis on implementation and management. It covers advanced integration technologies, service-oriented architecture (SOA), system security, and performance monitoring.

This course introduces the fundamental concepts, techniques, and methodologies used in data analytics for factual decision-making. It covers prescriptive analysis, predictive analysis, statistical procedures, and the practical implementation of data mining techniques.
